name: Prime Credit Time LLC
description_short: Prime Credit Time LLC is a credit repair service that disputes negative items on credit reports and provides credit education. They charge $0 upfront, $149 setup, then $99/month with no contracts.
description_long:
Prime Credit Time LLC is a credit repair and education services company operating across the United States. The company positions itself as a straightforward alternative to traditional credit repair by emphasizing transparency, no hidden fees, and compliance with federal regulations including the Credit Repair Organizations Act (CROA) and Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA).

The company's core service involves working with credit bureaus and creditors to challenge negative items on clients' credit reports. Clients enroll online through a secure portal, receive a personalized dispute strategy, and are assigned a dedicated case analyst. Prime Credit Time prepares and submits dispute letters on behalf of clients and provides 24/7 access to a client portal for real-time progress tracking. The service explicitly handles document preparation and guidance through the dispute process rather than legal representation.

Prime Credit Time distinguishes itself through its transparent pricing model ($0 start, $149 one-time setup, $99/month), 3-day refund policy on setup fees, and emphasis on online-only enrollment for compliance purposes. The website prominently features client testimonials citing score improvements (one claims 72 points in months, another reports reaching the 700s), though these are presented as individual results with a standard disclaimer that results vary and are not guaranteed.

The company operates within a heavily regulated industry and appropriately includes multiple disclaimers that they are not a law firm, do not provide legal advice, and that results are not guaranteed. However, like most credit repair services, Prime Credit Time's core value proposition—disputing items on credit reports—is theoretically available to consumers for free through direct disputes with credit bureaus, which is a material limitation worth considering.
address: 2585 S Bronco St, Las Vegas, NV 89146

company_info.headquarters: 2585 S Bronco St, Las Vegas, NV 89146

pros: Zero upfront cost ($0 due today) reduces entry barrier for cash-strapped consumers
3-day full refund policy on setup fees ($149) provides genuine risk mitigation
24/7 client portal access with real-time progress tracking and document upload capability
No phone sign-ups required—online-only enrollment reduces compliance and sales pressure concerns
Assigned dedicated case analyst rather than automated service
Explicit compliance mentions for CROA and FCRA throughout website
No contracts—can cancel at any time with refund eligibility within 3 days
cons: Monthly fee of $99 ($1,188/year) for services that consumers can theoretically perform free by disputing directly with credit bureaus
No specific information on dispute success rates, average timeline to results, or what percentage of disputed items are typically removed
Client testimonials are unverified anecdotes without methodology disclosure; claims like '72 points in months' lack context on starting score or number of items disputed
Website does not disclose company background, years in business, number of clients served, or management credentials
Disclaimer states 'results vary by individual and are not guaranteed,' but website leads with success story testimonials that suggest consistent positive outcomes
best_for: Consumers with documented errors on credit reports who lack time or confidence to dispute items themselves
Individuals working toward specific credit-dependent goals (home purchase, loan approval) on a timeline who want professional guidance
People overwhelmed by credit repair processes who value hand-holding and account management over cost optimization
diagnosis: Prime Credit Time is best for busy or overwhelmed consumers who want professional dispute management and accountability but can afford the $1,188 annual cost. The primary caveat is that all credit repair services operate in a commodity market—consumers have the legal right to dispute items themselves for free, so the value proposition depends entirely on whether paying for convenience, expertise, and accountability justifies the monthly fee relative to DIY disputing.
services: Preparation and submission of dispute letters to credit bureaus
Challenge of negative report items affecting credit score
Dedicated case analyst and advisor assignment
24/7 client portal access for progress tracking
Document upload and file management through secure portal
Credit education and guidance on credit maintenance
Real-time status updates on disputes and score improvements
Ongoing support and service continuation for sustained credit building
